introSection:
Steamin' Demon is a steel roller coaster located at Six Flags Great Escape in Queensbury, New York, USA. It was built by Arrow Dynamics and opened at its current location on May 26, 1984.

Steamin' Demon was the second roller coaster built at Six Flags Great Escape and the first with inversions.

sections.title: History
sections.content:
The ride first operated at Pontchartrain Beach in New Orleans, Louisiana, USA. It opened in 1978 and was called Ragin' Cajun. It closed in 1983 and was relocated to The Great Escape Fun Park in Queensbury, New York, where it opened the following year.

In 2016, the ride received virtual reality headgear showing a New Revolution film that many other Six Flags parks received.

sections.title: Design
sections.content: Steamin' Demon features a height of, a drop of and a max speed of.

sections.title: Color scheme
sections.content: The track is painted orange with yellow rails and the supports are painted blue. Previously, the track was painted orange. The track supports were green and the corkscrew supports yellow.

fullText:
Steamin' Demon is a steel roller coaster located at Six Flags Great Escape in Queensbury, New York, USA. It was built by Arrow Dynamics and opened at its current location on May 26, 1984.

Steamin' Demon was the second roller coaster built at Six Flags Great Escape and the first with inversions.

The ride first operated at Pontchartrain Beach in New Orleans, Louisiana, USA. It opened in 1978 and was called Ragin' Cajun. It closed in 1983 and was relocated to The Great Escape Fun Park in Queensbury, New York, where it opened the following year.

In 2016, the ride received virtual reality headgear showing a New Revolution film that many other Six Flags parks received.

Steamin' Demon features a height of, a drop of and a max speed of.

The track is painted orange with yellow rails and the supports are painted blue. Previously, the track was painted orange. The track supports were green and the corkscrew supports yellow.
